resources.string:
This application helps with consensual forensic analysis of Android devices to identify signs of compromise or surveillance. 

 The absence of detection from this app does not mean that your device is safe or secure.
Sorry, Bugbane is not supported on Android versions lower than 11 (API level 30). Please use AndroidQF or MVT from a trusted computer.
Connect to a Trusted Wi-Fi Network
To continue, connect your device to a protected Wi-Fi network, such as your home network or a personal hotspot.
To use this app, you need to enable notification permissions. \n\n Bugbane shows a notification so you can enter the pairing code for ADB Wireless Debugging.
To use this app, enable Developer Options in your device settings. \n\n The application acquires data using ADB Wireless Debugging, which is available only when Developer Options are enabled.
This phone is missing an Android security fix (CVE-2026-0073). While Bugbane scans, other people on the same Wi-Fi network could use that flaw to access the phone. Install system updates first.
Can\'t update now? Scan only while connected to a personal hotspot made by another phone or computer you trust — never on shared or public Wi-Fi. Turn Wireless Debugging off right after.
This device is missing the CVE-2026-0073 fix and Wireless Debugging is on, so it can be attacked over the network. Turn it off until your next acquisition — tap to open Developer Options.
Connect to Wi-Fi, enable ADB Wireless Debugging, tap “Pair device with pairing code,” then enter the code in the Bugbane notification.
Connect to Wi-Fi and enable ADB Wireless Debugging.
You\'re all set. You can now acquire data and run analyses, then share results with trusted third parties.
After each acquisition, it\'s recommended to disable Developer Options until the next session.
Warning: Acquisition is incomplete.\nYou can find it in the Acquisitions screen.\nFailed modules: %1$s
This is a beta version: use it at your own risk. Please report any bugs on GitHub.
Configure your preferences and app settings here.
It\'s recommended to disable Developer Options after each session and re-enable them only when needed.
The indicators of compromise (IoCs) are used to identify signs of infections or surveillance on Android devices. They are updated periodically to ensure the accuracy of the analysis.
Bugbane is an open-source project developed by Osservatorio Nessuno OdV.
Shown when Bugbane crashes so you can send a report to the developers.
Tap to send a crash report by email.
Bugbane crashed — tap to send a report
A crash report is attached. Please describe what you were doing when the app crashed.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s? This action cannot be undone.
No acquisitions yet. Perform one to see it here.
ADB pairing service found on port %d
The exported archive contains potentially private information and has been encrypted. The password will be shown only once:\n\n%1$s
Confirm your screen lock to protect acquisition data on this device
Confirm your screen lock to read this acquisition
Screen lock confirmed. Now enter the acquisition password to finish unlocking.

Acquisitions are encrypted on this device. Locking the encryption key to your fingerprint or screen lock means that even a forensic tool with full access to this phone can\'t read them without you.\n\nChanging your PIN or password later is fine, but if you ever remove your screen lock the key is erased and acquisitions can\'t be recovered. To keep them across a lock removal, set an acquisition password in Settings.
Couldn\'t set up acquisition protection. Please try again.

This device has no screen lock, so acquisitions can only be protected by a password you choose. You\'ll need it to open or export acquisitions later — it can\'t be recovered. If you leave without setting one, this acquisition is discarded.
Add a password for stronger protection. On this device a password keeps acquisitions safe even against a sophisticated forensic tool — beyond what the fingerprint alone can do.

Optionally add a password on top of your fingerprint. It\'s asked together with your fingerprint when reading acquisitions, and protects them even against an attacker who fully compromises this phone or learns your PIN. It also keeps your acquisitions readable if you ever remove your screen lock.
Acquisitions can no longer be unlocked

Your screen lock was removed, so the key that protected your acquisitions was erased by Android and they can no longer be decrypted. (Changing your PIN or password is fine — only removing the lock erases the key.)\n\nReset protection to keep using Bugbane. The old acquisitions are unreadable and will be removed.
Set up protection again to keep using Bugbane. Add a screen lock to protect the key with your fingerprint, or set an acquisition password that stays valid even if the lock is later removed.

Choose an acquisition password. You\'ll enter it to open or export acquisitions — it can\'t be recovered, so store it safely. A password keeps your acquisitions protected even if the screen lock is later removed.
You\'ll confirm your screen lock to save the password.
Couldn\'t set the password. Please try again.

Without a password, acquisitions are protected only by your screen lock. A forensic tool that exploits this phone, or anyone who learns your PIN, may be able to read them. Make sure your screen lock is a strong PIN or password — at least 10 characters — and prefer letters and numbers over a short numeric PIN.
Change the password that protects your acquisitions. Existing acquisitions stay readable; only the protection changes.
You\'ll confirm your screen lock twice.
Require a password in addition to your fingerprint or screen lock. Both will be needed to open acquisitions.
Keep only the fingerprint / screen-lock requirement.
Protect with the password alone. Unlike the fingerprint, this survives removing your screen lock — do it before removing the lock if you want to keep your acquisitions.
Are you sure you want to delete this analysis? This action cannot be undone.
Potential spyware indicators were found in this analysis. For help and support, consider contacting a trusted helpline.

Osservatorio Nessuno OdV is a registered non-profit Italian organization, run entirely by volunteers. We are committed to defending the right to privacy, anonymity, freedom of information, expression, and communication, as well as digital rights more broadly.
Bugbane has been developed by Osservatorio Nessuno OdV with the help of the following volunteers:
Analysis finished. Tap to view the results
Some modules were skipped. Tap to view the results.
The following traces of compromission were found:
Fingerprints of past devices connected via Android Debug Bridge (ADB) were found:
The following apps have risky permissions set:
The following root-linked apps and packages were found:
This phone has not received security updates for more than six months. Last update:
The following system partitions were mounted as read-write (rw). This may indicate system modifications.
The following partitions have suspicious mount options:
The following dangerous settings were found:
The following suspicious crashes were found:
The following files were found at suspicious paths:
Packages installed via adb or insecure method
The following packages were installed via adb or another insecure method:
Packages installed via a third party store
The following packages were installed via a third party store:
The following packages were installed via a browser:
The following security-related packages were disabled:
The following system OTA update packages were disabled:
The following root software binaries were found:
Receivers to intercept outgoing SMS messages
The following receivers were found to intercept outgoing SMS messages:
Receivers to intercept incoming SMS messages
The following receivers were found to intercept incoming SMS messages:
Receivers to intercept incoming data SMS messages
The following receivers were found to intercept incoming data SMS messages:
The following receivers were found to monitor telephony state/incoming calls:
The following receivers were found to monitor outgoing calls:
The following potentially suspicious ANR crashes were found:
